title: Preferred Tech Defaults Preset
description: Uses Rank 1 (default/preferred) choices for all categories - Andrew Hopper's preferred stack
note: This preset explicitly sets all Rank 1 defaults. Use for prototypes that should follow standard preferences exactly. Generally not needed since Rank 1 is default, but useful for documentation or when overriding a parent .tech-preferences.local.json file.
description_extended: This preset represents the 'golden path' tech stack - battle-tested, well-documented, and recommended for most projects. It uses shadcn/ui for components, Next.js for full-stack apps, Deepgram for ASR, Claude for LLMs, PostHog for analytics, etc.

preferences.name: Next.js
preferences.rationale: Full-stack React with App Router, Server Components, built-in API routes, excellent DX, React 19 stable support

preferences.name: shadcn/ui
preferences.rationale: Copy-paste components, Radix UI primitives, Tailwind styling, full customization

preferences.name: Yjs
preferences.rationale: CRDT-based, framework-agnostic, peer-to-peer sync, conflict-free merging

preferences.name: Tailwind CSS
preferences.rationale: Utility-first, fast development, consistent design, excellent DX with IntelliSense

preferences.name: Next.js API Routes
preferences.rationale: Integrated with frontend, serverless-ready, TypeScript, simple deploy

preferences.name: PostgreSQL
preferences.rationale: Industry standard, ACID, JSON support, full-text search, mature ecosystem

preferences.name: Drizzle ORM
preferences.rationale: TypeScript-first, SQL-like syntax, lightweight, excellent DX, type-safe

preferences.name: Deepgram
preferences.rationale: Fast, accurate, real-time streaming, speaker diarization, affordable, great API

preferences.name: ElevenLabs
preferences.rationale: Best quality, natural voices, voice cloning, multilingual, API + SDKs

preferences.name: Claude Vision (Anthropic)
preferences.rationale: Multimodal LLM, understands context, charts, diagrams, handwriting, reasoning

preferences.name: AWS Bedrock (Claude)
preferences.rationale: AWS-native, no data retention, enterprise compliance, VPC support

preferences.name: BAML
preferences.rationale: Type-safe LLM framework, Pydantic integration, compile-time validation, structured outputs

preferences.name: AWS Bedrock (Titan Embeddings)
preferences.rationale: AWS-native, 8K context, multilingual, cost-effective, no data retention

preferences.name: pgvector (PostgreSQL)
preferences.rationale: PostgreSQL extension, no new infrastructure, ACID guarantees, mature

preferences.name: Playwright
preferences.rationale: Cross-browser, fast, reliable, auto-wait, parallel execution, great DX

preferences.name: Vitest
preferences.rationale: Vite-native, fast, Jest-compatible API, ESM first, watch mode

preferences.name: AWS Amplify
preferences.rationale: AWS-native, branch previews, SSR support, env vars, CI/CD

preferences.name: AWS Lambda + API Gateway
preferences.rationale: Serverless, auto-scaling, pay-per-use, AWS ecosystem integration

preferences.name: Sentry
preferences.rationale: Error tracking, performance monitoring, release tracking, source maps

preferences.name: GitHub Actions
preferences.rationale: Native to GitHub, matrix builds, reusable workflows, marketplace

preferences.name: Clerk
preferences.rationale: Complete auth solution, beautiful UI, Next.js integration, user mgmt

preferences.name: Resend
preferences.rationale: Developer-first, React Email support, simple API, excellent DX

preferences.name: Stripe
preferences.rationale: Industry standard, excellent API, Stripe Elements, webhooks, subscriptions

preferences.name: AWS S3
preferences.rationale: AWS-native, 11 9s durability, versioning, lifecycle policies, cost tiers

preferences.name: PostHog
preferences.rationale: Self-hosted or cloud, feature flags, session replay, privacy-friendly

preferences.name: Redis (Upstash)
preferences.rationale: Serverless Redis, per-request pricing, REST API, global replication

preferences.name: Algolia
preferences.rationale: Fast, typo-tolerance, faceting, instant search, great DX

preferences.name: Inngest
preferences.rationale: Serverless, event-driven, durable execution, retries, type-safe, great DX

preferences.name: Temporal
preferences.rationale: Durable execution, workflow-as-code, retries, versioning, excellent DX

preferences.name: AWS CDK
preferences.rationale: AWS-native, TypeScript/Python, high-level constructs, CloudFormation under the hood

preferences.name: dbt (Data Build Tool)
preferences.rationale: SQL-based transformations, version control, testing, documentation, modular models

preferences.name: AWS Redshift
preferences.rationale: AWS-native, columnar storage, scalable, SQL interface

preferences.name: AWS S3 + Lake Formation
preferences.rationale: AWS-native, fine-grained access control, integrated with Glue/Athena

preferences.name: Recharts
preferences.rationale: React-native, composable, responsive, simple API, good defaults

preferences.name: Mermaid
preferences.rationale: Text-based, simple syntax, GitHub integration, version-controllable, no layout

preferences.name: OpenAPI 3.0 (Swagger)
preferences.rationale: Industry standard, auto docs, Swagger UI, code generation

preferences.name: Astro (Starlight)
preferences.rationale: Developer-first, MDX support, fast builds, component islands, Git-based

preferences.name: Notion
preferences.rationale: All-in-one workspace, databases, wikis, collaboration, AI features

preferences.name: Astro
preferences.rationale: Fast, component islands, MDX, framework-agnostic, excellent DX
